skill.name: Apple HIG: Technologies
skill.description: Apple Human Interface Guidelines for technology integrations including Siri, Apple Pay, HealthKit, HomeKit, ARKit, and more
skill.summary: Comprehensive HIG reference for integrating Apple technologies with proper privacy, accessibility, and user experience patterns
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill contains only markdown documentation files referencing Apple's Human Interface Guidelines. All 2880 static analysis findings are false positives - they are pattern matches in documentation text (URLs to Apple developer docs, code examples in markdown blocks, technical terminology) rather than executable code. No scripts, network calls, or system access present. Safe for publication.

content.user_title: Apply Apple HIG standards to technology integrations
content.value_statement: Developers struggle to implement Apple technologies correctly while meeting App Store guidelines. This skill provides authoritative HIG guidance for Siri, Apple Pay, HealthKit, and 25+ Apple frameworks to ensure approval-ready implementations.
actual_capabilities: Provide HIG-compliant implementation checklists for 28 Apple technologies
Generate privacy and permission requirement documentation
Create user flow diagrams from permission prompts to task completion
Identify required versus optional features for App Store approval
Supply testing guidance including edge cases for each technology
Cross-reference related HIG topics like inputs, components, and status indicators
limitations: Provides guidelines only - does not generate working code implementations
Cannot verify current API changes beyond documented HIG versions
Does not replace official Apple developer documentation or WWDC sessions
Focuses on iOS/macOS/watchOS/tvOS - limited web implementation guidance

use_cases.title: Siri Integration Planning
use_cases.description: Define Intents, App Shortcuts, and voice interaction patterns that meet Apple's conversational design standards

use_cases.title: Apple Pay Checkout Implementation
use_cases.description: Implement payment flows with correct button styles, authentication, and security requirements for App Store approval
use_cases.target_user: E-commerce app developer adding payment options

use_cases.title: HealthKit Permission Design
use_cases.description: Create privacy-compliant health data access patterns with proper usage descriptions and user consent flows

prompt_templates.title: Basic Technology Guidelines
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the Apple HIG requirements for integrating [technology name] into my app? List the key principles and must-follow guidelines.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with a new Apple technology

prompt_templates.title: Implementation Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a step-by-step implementation checklist for [specific feature] following Apple HIG. Include required vs optional features and common rejection reasons.

prompt_templates.title: Privacy and Permissions
prompt_templates.prompt: What privacy requirements and permission prompts do I need for [technology]? Provide example usage description strings and explain what data access requires user consent.

prompt_templates.title: App Store Review Preparation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my [technology] implementation plan against HIG guidelines. Identify any patterns that could cause App Store rejection and suggest corrections.

output_examples.input: What are the Siri integration requirements for my food ordering app?
output: 1. Define Intents for: SearchForMenuItems, OrderFood, TrackOrder
2. Create App Shortcuts for common actions (reorder favorite)
3. Write natural conversational phrases for each intent
4. Confirm all parameters before executing order
5. Handle errors with clear fallback options
Privacy: Include NSUserTrackingUsageDescription if using order history for suggestions

output_examples.input: How should I design Apple Pay button placement in my checkout flow?
output: - Display at same visual prominence as other payment methods
- Use Apple-provided PKPaymentButton API (not custom images)
- Place before credit card form fields, not after
- Support both 'Buy with Apple Pay' (direct) and standard button
- Never hide or gray-out the button - explain unavailability after tap if needed
- Required: Standard Apple Pay button styles only

best_practices: Always check for .claude/apple-design-context.md before asking questions - use existing context first
Request only the minimum data permissions needed and explain the user benefit before each permission prompt
Test all accessibility requirements including VoiceOver labels, Dynamic Type, and Switch Control compatibility
anti_patterns: Do not create custom payment button designs - Apple Pay buttons must use Apple-provided APIs
Never request health data without explaining the specific health benefit to the user first
Avoid AR experiences that feel gimmicky - only use when spatial context provides genuine value

faq.question: Which Apple technologies does this skill cover?
faq.answer: 28 technologies including Siri, Apple Pay, HealthKit, HomeKit, ARKit, Core ML, Generative AI, iCloud, Sign in with Apple, SharePlay, CarPlay, Game Center, In-App Purchase, NFC, Wallet, VoiceOver, Maps, Mac Catalyst, and more.

faq.question: Does this skill generate working code?
faq.answer: No - it provides HIG guidelines, implementation checklists, and design patterns. Use it alongside coding skills to implement the recommended patterns.

faq.question: How do I know if my App Store submission will be approved?
faq.answer: This skill identifies HIG compliance requirements and common rejection reasons. Final approval depends on Apple's review team and current guidelines.

faq.question: Can this help with web implementations of Apple technologies?
faq.answer: Partially. Apple Pay on Web and Sign in with Apple have web-specific guidance included, but the focus is native iOS/macOS/watchOS/tvOS development.

faq.question: Does this cover the latest iOS version requirements?
faq.answer: The skill references HIG documentation which is updated by Apple. Always verify current requirements in official Apple Developer Documentation for the latest iOS version.

faq.question: What should I do before using this skill?
faq.answer: Check for .claude/apple-design-context.md in your project. This file may contain additional context that reduces the need for follow-up questions.
